references.title: The Monetization Playbook We Used at Eventbrite
references.summary: One of the problems we faced when I joined Eventbrite was that Eventbrite had a pretty low take rate, meaning when event creators sold paid tickets on our platform, we took a very low percentage. And when creators sold free tickets on the platform, which are the majority of tickets, we didn’t make any money. You need an incredible amount of volume to make profits with low take rates, like say Paypal or Stripe, and even though Eventbrite has tremendous scale, it wasn’t enough to make material profits. We needed a way to make more money for the value we created if we wanted to be a successful company i.e. a new monetization playbook. I’ll discuss below the playbook we built, and some of the process to get there.

references.title: Fintech Product Management Bootcamp 2026
references.summary:
Whether you're dreaming of becoming a product manager, already working in fintech, or just curious about managing techy finance products, this course is your perfect match. We've packed it with all the juicy details you need to know about finance, tech, and how to manage fintech products like a pro. Get ready to learn a ton and tackle the fintech world with confidence!


What You'll Learn:

  1. Foundations of Product Management: Understand the core principles of product management, including the roles, responsibilities, and daily activities of a product manager within fintech companies like GRAB and Stripe.

  2. Introduction to Fintech: Gain a solid understanding of the financial technology sector, including banking operations, payment systems, and money instruments.

  3. Global Payment Infrastructure: Explore the payment infrastructures across different countries, including open banking in the UK, UPI in India, and card networks in the USA.

  4. License, Safety, and Security: Learn about payment processors, gateways, banking licenses, compliance, customer security, fraud prevention, and the intricacies of cross-border payments.

  5. Fintech Application Spectrum: Discover the various types of fintech apps, from lending and borrowing platforms to neobanks and investment platforms, and understand their business models.

  6. Advanced Payment Technologies: Delve into NFC, decentralized finance (DeFi), e-money, e-wallets, and Banking as a Service (BaaS), including the evolution from Web 1.0 to Web 3.0.

  7. Product Strategy for Fintech: Develop a compelling product strategy for fintech apps, including product vision, differentiation, and goal setting using the OKR framework.

  8. Product Roadmap and Agile Planning: Master the skills of creating agile product roadmaps, managing product backlogs, and executing sprint planning using tools like JIRA.

  9. Product Analytics: Leverage product analytics for strategic decision-making, learning to use tools like Mixpanel and Google Analytics to understand user behavior and measure product value.

  10. Technical Skills for Product Managers: Acquire essential technical skills, including an introduction to internet workings, APIs, webpage components, and building web apps.

Course Structure:

Section 1: Introduction to Product Management

  • Course Outline

  • What is a Product?

  • Who is a Product Manager?

  • Product Team at GRAB and Stripe

  • What is Product Management?

  • Unveiling What PMs Actually Do

  • Let's Solve a Problem

  • Product Team Dynamics

  • Product Development Process

  • Let's Pick a Product

  • Introduction to Course Workbook

  • Assignment 1: Download and solve the workbook to solidify your understanding.

Section 2: Entering the Fintech Universe

Explore the essentials of finance and technology, unravel the workings of banks, and payment systems, and dive into the world of fintech innovations.

  • Module Outline

  • What is a Bank and How Does it Work?

  • Basics of Payment Systems

  • Types of Money Instruments

  • Assignment 2: Engage with an exercise to understand money instruments in depth.

Section 3: Global Payment Infrastructure

Gain a global perspective on payment infrastructure, understanding the key systems in place across different countries.

  • Payment Infrastructure Across Countries

  • Open Banking Infrastructure in UK

  • UPI Infrastructure in India

  • Cards Network in the USA

  • Assignment 3: Analyze the payment infrastructures of different countries.

Section 4: Security and Compliance in Fintech

Learn about the critical aspects of licensing, security, and compliance in the fintech sector, including customer safety and fraud prevention.

  • Introduction to Payment Processor and Payment Gateway

  • Banking License and Compliance

  • Customer Security & Safety

  • Fraud Prevention and Detection

  • Disputes and Chargebacks

  • Payment Instruments and Types of Cards

  • Cross-Border Payment System Using SWIFT

  • Direct Debit for Recurring Payments

  • ACH (Automated Clearing House)

  • Assignment 4: Dive into a case study on fraud prevention.

Section 5: Exploring Fintech Applications

Discover the various types of fintech applications, from lending platforms to insuretech, and learn how these innovations are reshaping finance.

  • Intro to Fintech Apps

  • Lending and Borrowing Platforms

  • Embedded Finance

  • Introduction to Payment Gateway

  • Insurance Tech (InsureTech) Platform

  • Investment Platform

  • How Neobanks Make Money

  • Assignment 5: Conceptualize your fintech application.

Section 6: Advanced Payment Technologies

Stay ahead with insights into advanced payment technologies and the future of finance with decentralized finance, e-money, and more.

  • Advanced Payment Technology (NFC)

  • Decentralized Finance (DeFi)

  • E-money and E-wallets

  • Banking as a Service (BaaS)

  • Web 1.0, Web 2.0, Web 3.0

  • Assignment 6: Explore a DeFi project.

Section 7: Crafting Your Fintech Product Strategy

Develop a comprehensive product strategy for fintech applications, from outlining problems to defining success metrics.

  • Pick Your Product for Course Workbook

  • Introduction to Product Strategy

  • Product Strategy Stack for Fintech App

  • Product Vision and Vision Statement

  • Product Vision Board

  • User Persona and Empathy Map

  • User Journey Map

  • Jobs-to-be-Done

  • Market Size Estimation

  • OKRs for Fintech App

  • Product Differentiators and Success Metrics

  • Feature Breakdown List

  • Summarizing Product Strategy

  • Assignment 7: Develop a product strategy for your fintech application.

Section 8: Product Roadmap and Planning

Navigate through creating agile roadmaps, understand scrum methodologies, and get hands-on with planning and managing product releases.

  • Go Product Roadmap

  • Build Your Agile Roadmap

  • Brainstorming Sessions

  • Product Requirement Document (PRD)

  • Assignment 8: Create an agile roadmap for a fintech product.

Section 9: Leveraging Analytics for Fintech Products

Unlock the power of product analytics for fintech, learning to use data to make informed decisions and measure product success.

  • Introduction to Product Analytics

  • User Behavior Tracking

  • Marketing Analytics vs. Product Analytics

  • Mixpanel for Fintech

  • Measuring Product Value

Section 10: Mastering Excel for Product Management

Enhance your analytical skills with Microsoft Excel, masteringpivot tables, vlookup, and chart creation to present and analyze product data effectively.

  • Introduction to Microsoft Excel

  • Pivot Tables in Excel

  • Vlookup in Excel

  • Chart Mastery for Product Data

  • Assignment 10: Craft a data analysis report using Excel.

Section 11: Technology Insights for Product Managers

Demystify the tech behind fintech products. Understand internet fundamentals, web development, and how APIs fuel fintech innovations.

  • How the Internet Works

  • DNS and ISPs Explained

  • Client-Server Model

  • Understanding Internet Protocols

  • Webpage Components

  • Dive into HTML, CSS, and JavaScript

  • Introduction to Figma and UI Design

  • Building a Weather App with Web Technologies

  • APIs and Postman for Beginners

  • Assignment 11: Build your fintech web app prototype.


Who This Course Is For

This course is designed for individuals at various stages of their careers who are looking to specialize in fintech product management, including:

  • Aspiring product managers seeking to enter the fintech industry.

  • Existing product managers in the fintech space looking to deepen their expertise.

  • Software developers, business analysts, UX/UI designers, and other professionals transitioning to fintech product management roles.

  • Fintech entrepreneurs and startup founders aiming to build competitive and innovative products.

  • Anyone with a keen interest in financial technology, product strategy, and agile methodologies.
